description: Phase 4 self-host: close the front-end gaps, stand up the 3-stage bootstrap + byte-identical fixpoint, then port the compiler proper (token..backend, then typecheck) to goal leaf-to-root until a goal-built compiler compiles itself and passes the corpus. See SELF-HOST-RESEARCH.md.

userStories.title: Fix iota const-block miscompile
userStories.description: As the self-host effort, I need idiomatic Go enum const blocks to transpile correctly so that the compiler's own iota-based declarations (e.g. internal/token) survive a round-trip.
acceptanceCriteria: Transpiling `const ( Red Color = iota; Green; Blue )` emits Go where Green and Blue are bare const names that repeat the iota initializer, NOT `Green Blue` collapsed into a name+type
The transpiled output of an idiomatic iota enum compiles with `go build`
A regression test (corpus transpile case or backend test) pins the corrected emission and fails on the old name+type collapse
userStories.notes: GATING — confirmed silent miscompile (exit 0): `const(Red Color=iota; Green; Blue)` -> `const(Red Color=iota; Green Blue)`, losing Blue's value. Lives in the parser GenDecl/ValueSpec path + backend const emission. Nothing self-hosts until this is fixed; internal/token depends on it.

userStories.title: Add per-package transpile-and-build smoke gate
userStories.description: As the self-host effort, I need a gate that transpiles each in-scope compiler package and compiles the output so that silent transpile defects (which the checker does not flag) are caught by `go build`.
acceptanceCriteria: A test or `task` target transpiles each in-scope package's source through the goal front-end and runs `go build` (and where practical the package's existing tests) on the generated Go
The gate covers at least token, lexer, ast, parser, sema, project, pipeline, backend
The gate is green after US-001 and fails if any covered package transpiles to non-compiling Go
userStories.notes: The compiler source is already valid goal (Go superset), so the gate can feed internal/<pkg>/*.go (as .goal) through goalc/goal build. This is the verification harness every later port story reuses. `go build` is the real gate because the checker stays silent on miscompiles (that is how US-001 was found).

userStories.title: Support generic function declarations
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need top-level generic function declarations to parse and transpile so that the language is complete enough to express any compiler code without restriction.
acceptanceCriteria: `func Identity[T any](x T) T { return x }` parses without error (no `expected (, found [`)
ast.FuncType carries a TypeParams field and the backend emits the type-parameter list
The generic function transpiles to valid Go that `go build` accepts, with a test covering a constrained param (e.g. [T comparable])
userStories.notes: Localized ~3-spot fix: parser.go:347 parseFuncDecl must call the existing atTypeParams/parseTypeParams (parser.go:281,296,307); add TypeParams to ast.FuncType (only TypeSpec has one, ast.go:261); emit in funcDecl/funcSig (backend/emit.go:360,439). Compiler uses zero generic funcs, so this is completeness, not gating.

userStories.title: Stand up bootstrap and fixpoint harness
userStories.description: As the self-host effort, I need the 3-stage bootstrap and byte-identical fixpoint check wired as repeatable targets so that every ported package can be validated against the trust proof from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: A `selfhost/` directory holds a `package main` goal program (a goalc/goal-build equivalent), even if initially a thin skeleton
Taskfile `bootstrap` and `fixpoint` targets run the stage-0 -> goal-c-1 -> goal-c-2 sequence and `diff -r` the Go emitted by goal-c-1 and goal-c-2 for the compiler's own source
Running the fixpoint target exits 0 (byte-identical) against the current skeleton
userStories.notes: No selfhost dir or bootstrap target exists today. Stage-0 = ./bin/goal[c]; goal-c-1 = goal-written compiler built by stage-0; goal-c-2 = built by goal-c-1; fixpoint = goal-c-1 and goal-c-2 emit identical Go. CLI contract must match stage-0 (build --emit / stdin goalc). See SELF-HOST-RESEARCH.md §5.

userStories.title: Port token package to goal
userStories.description: As the self-hosted compiler, I need internal/token reimplemented in goal so that the leaf of the dependency DAG is goal-native.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/token holds the token package as goal source
It transpiles via the smoke gate (US-002) and the generated Go compiles
The existing token tests pass against the transpiled package
userStories.notes: 361 LOC, leaf (no internal deps). Exercises the US-001 iota fix directly (token kinds are an iota enum).

userStories.title: Port lexer package to goal
userStories.description: As the self-hosted compiler, I need internal/lexer reimplemented in goal so that tokenization is goal-native.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/lexer holds the lexer as goal source importing the ported token package
It transpiles and the generated Go compiles (unicode, unicode/utf8 pass through)
The existing lexer tests pass against the transpiled package
userStories.notes: 514 LOC; depends on token. Foreign imports unicode/unicode/utf8 pass through.

userStories.title: Port ast package to goal
userStories.description: As the self-hosted compiler, I need internal/ast reimplemented in goal so that node definitions and Walk are goal-native.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/ast holds the ast package as goal source importing the ported token package
It transpiles and the generated Go compiles
The existing ast tests pass against the transpiled package
userStories.notes: 1,988 LOC; depends on token. dump.go uses reflect for debug Sexpr — drop it from the self-hosted build or rewrite as an explicit node switch (not on the compile path).

userStories.title: Port parser package to goal
userStories.description: As the self-hosted compiler, I need internal/parser reimplemented in goal so that source-to-AST is goal-native.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/parser holds the parser as goal source importing the ported token, lexer, ast packages
It transpiles and the generated Go compiles
The existing parser tests pass against the transpiled package
userStories.notes: 1,765 LOC; hand-written recursive descent with precedence climbing. Depends on token, lexer, ast. May be large for one context window — if so, split by parser.go vs goal_*.go.

userStories.title: Port sema package to goal
userStories.description: As the self-hosted compiler, I need internal/sema reimplemented in goal so that resolution and checking are goal-native.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/sema holds the sema package as goal source importing the ported token, ast, parser packages
It transpiles and the generated Go compiles (go/parser, go/format, go/types pass through as foreign imports)
The existing sema tests pass against the transpiled package
userStories.notes: 2,695 LOC; multi-pass resolve + check + foreign + ?-inference. foreign.go imports go/parser/go/format/go/types — confirmed they pass through. Large; may need splitting by file group (resolve, check-family, foreign).

userStories.title: Port project and pipeline packages to goal
userStories.description: As the self-hosted compiler, I need internal/project and internal/pipeline reimplemented in goal so that discovery and output types are goal-native.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/project and selfhost/pipeline hold those packages as goal source
Both transpile and the generated Go compiles (io/fs, os, path/filepath pass through)
The existing project and pipeline tests pass against the transpiled packages
userStories.notes: Small (147 + 173 LOC), combined into one story. project depends on parser; pipeline is output types + source map.

userStories.title: Port backend package to goal
userStories.description: As the self-hosted compiler, I need internal/backend reimplemented in goal so that AST-to-Go emission is goal-native.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/backend holds the backend as goal source importing the ported token, ast, parser, sema, project, pipeline packages
It transpiles and the generated Go compiles (go/format, go/types, go/importer pass through)
The existing backend tests and the corpus transpile + behavioral tiers pass against the transpiled backend
userStories.notes: 3,441 LOC; the largest and gnarliest (stateful emitter, ~100 methods, all goal-specific lowerings). Ported last against a fully working ported front-end, with the corpus as control. Likely needs splitting by emit.go vs lower.go vs the rest.

userStories.title: Self-host the build path and prove fixpoint
userStories.description: As the self-host effort, I need the goal-written compiler to compile itself so that the byte-identical fixpoint trust proof holds for the build path.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/ exposes a goal-written goalc/goal-build main wired to the ported token..backend packages
The US-004 fixpoint target passes: goal-c-1 and goal-c-2 emit byte-identical Go for the compiler's own source
The goal-built compiler passes the corpus transpile + behavioral conformance tiers
userStories.notes: The milestone. Covers the build/transpile path (token..backend); typecheck (the goal check depth stage) follows in US-013. This is where self-host is actually achieved for the transpiler.

userStories.title: Port typecheck and self-host goal check
userStories.description: As the self-hosted compiler, I need internal/typecheck reimplemented in goal so that the depth-check path of `goal check` is also goal-native.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/typecheck holds the typecheck package as goal source (go/types, go/importer, go/ast, go/parser pass through as foreign imports)
It transpiles, the generated Go compiles, and the existing typecheck depth tests pass against it
The goal-built compiler runs `goal check` over the corpus check cases with the same diagnostics as the Go-built compiler, and the fixpoint still holds with typecheck included
userStories.notes: 772 LOC; deferrable past the build-path fixpoint (US-012). Extends self-host from `goal build` to `goal check`. go/types et al. import as passthrough — no shelling out needed.
